title: Fusion LCOE vs. Solar+Storage and Fission (2025-2050)
required_metrics: Solar+storage LCOE projections (USD/MWh) for same years
Fission LCOE projections (USD/MWh) for same years
preferred_sources: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Analysis
Company roadmaps (Commonwealth Fusion Systems, TAE Technologies)
search_queries: IEA World Energy Outlook 2024 LCOE nuclear
data_quality_rule: Use public projections from authoritative sources; if fusion LCOE is not available, note as 'not yet published' and use company targets with uncertainty bands.

title: Fusion Funding by Stage (2015-2024)
executive_question: Is fusion investment maturing or still speculative?
required_metrics: Annual venture capital funding ($M) for fusion startups, broken down by seed, Series A, Series B, Series C+
Number of fusion startups reaching Series C or beyond
search_queries: fusion energy venture capital funding 2024 PitchBook
data_quality_rule: Cross-reference PitchBook and Crunchbase; if data is behind paywall, use Fusion Industry Association public summaries.

title: Tritium Supply vs. Demand for Fusion Fleet (2030-2050)
executive_question: Can tritium supply support significant fusion capacity?
required_metrics: Global tritium production capacity (kg/year) from CANDU reactors and other sources, 2025-2050
Projected tritium demand (kg/year) for fusion fleet scenarios (e.g., 1 GW, 10 GW, 50 GW installed capacity)
Tritium consumption rate per fusion plant (kg/GW-year)
comparison_set: Supply vs. demand under different deployment scenarios
preferred_sources: Fusion engineering papers (e.g., Tritium Breeding Ratio requirements)
data_quality_rule: Use publicly available IAEA data; if CANDU production is proprietary, use estimates from academic literature. Note uncertainty ranges.

title: Timeline Comparison: ITER, SPARC, CFETR, DEMO
executive_question: What are the realistic milestones for first fusion power?
required_metrics: Key milestones and target dates for ITER (first plasma, DT operation, full power)
Key milestones for SPARC (construction start, first plasma, Q>1 demonstration)
Key milestones for CFETR (design completion, construction start, operation)
Key milestones for DEMO (conceptual design, engineering design, construction)
data_quality_rule: Use official project announcements; if dates are delayed, note the most recent update. Include historical delays as annotations.

title: Regulatory Licensing Timeline: Fusion vs. Fission
executive_question: Does fusion have a regulatory advantage?
required_metrics: Average licensing timeline (months) for fusion reactors (e.g., from application to approval) in US, UK, Canada
Average licensing timeline (months) for fission reactors (e.g., small modular reactors) in same countries
comparison_set: Fusion vs. fission licensing duration by country
preferred_sources: US Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) fusion regulatory framework documents
UK Environment Agency fusion licensing guidance
Canadian Nuclear Safety Commission (CNSC) pre-licensing reviews
data_quality_rule: Use official regulatory documents; if fusion licensing timelines are not yet established, note as 'in development' and use fission timelines for comparison.

title: Industrial Heat Demand by Temperature Range (PJ/year)
executive_question: Which industrial sectors could be early fusion adopters?
required_metrics: Industrial heat demand (PJ/year) by temperature range: low (<150°C), medium (150-400°C), high (400-800°C), very high (>800°C)
Sector breakdown: steel, cement, chemicals, refining, etc.
comparison_set: Heat demand by temperature and sector
search_queries: IEA industrial heat demand by temperature 2024
US DOE industrial heat decarbonization roadmap
data_quality_rule: Use IEA or DOE public datasets; if temperature breakdown is not available, use sector-level data and estimate temperature ranges.

title: Government Fusion R&D Budgets by Country (USD/year)
executive_question: Which countries are investing most in fusion?
required_metrics: Annual government fusion R&D budget (USD) for US, EU (including Euratom), China, Japan, South Korea, UK, Russia, India
Breakdown by program (e.g., ITER contribution, domestic programs)
preferred_sources: US DOE Fusion Energy Sciences budget documents
China Ministry of Science and Technology announcements
Japan Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology (MEXT)
data_quality_rule: Use official government budget documents; if China budget is not publicly available, use estimates from academic papers or state media. Convert all to USD using average exchange rates.
